title: Making sense of lyrics
wikitext:
[]: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Apr 10, 2016 3:25 pm It was established in previous threads that the lyrics of certain tracks in NTE mirror that of what we're seeing on screen, be it Wunder's launch singing of humanity's strength, Ode to Joy of Seele's delight at achieving divinity, and The Wrath of God in All His Fury mirroring the sense of fear and hopelessness Wille is feeling when they find that Eva-13 has awoken. But is this true for all the tracks in NTE? How exactly does God's message tie into the space battle between Asuka and the Mark-4 at the beginning of Q for example?

[]: Re: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Apr 13, 2016 9:53 am Well there was that one moment that Evageeks had. When it was thought that the DSS choker around Shinji's neck was translated into that one piano solo that plays when Shinji trips out about not saving Rei. I think that was debunked though... I know this thread title is about lyrics but I think it can definitely be broadened to the soundtrack of Q as a whole. The new slow guitar theme that plays when Shinji is speaking with Rei Q is absolutely hopeless without using any lyrics. And 13's activation was based on Shinji and Kaworu's piano mashup, which didn't really have any lyrics either.

[]: Re: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Apr 16, 2016 12:46 pm Here are the lyrical tracks and their corresponding scenes in the movies: 1.0 Showdown: Unit 01 vs. Shamshel. lyrics  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sat Apr 16, 2016 1:02 pm Super thanks for putting that all together. Why do you suppose singing about the DSS would be relevant in a space battle between Unit-02 and the Mark-04b for the tesseract?

[]: Re: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Apr 16, 2016 1:50 pm You're welcome! I'm not sure it has anything to do with the fight. It's great for setting the mood and the tone of the scene, though. Showdown's in that same boat, I think. The lyrics are super dramatic for an Angel that's just a filler boss. Or Seele could have put 4B there as part of their plan. Who knows?

[]: Re: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sat Apr 16, 2016 2:00 pm Given that Unit-01 was apart of the scene, coupled with how God lyrics are Adam-linked, would it not make more sense for the track to have been given a more "A God is awakening" vibe to it?, since that's pretty much what happens at the end of the scene

[]: Making sense of lyrics             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Apr 10, 2016 3:25 pm It was established in previous threads that the lyrics of certain tracks in NTE mirror that of what we're seeing on screen, be it Wunder's launch singing of humanity's strength, Ode to Joy of Seele's delight at achieving divinity, and The Wrath of God in All His Fury mirroring the sense of fear and hopelessness Wille is feeling when they find that Eva-13 has awoken. But is this true for all the tracks in NTE? How exactly does God's message tie into the space battle between Asuka and the Mark-4 at the beginning of Q for example?

I know this thread title is about lyrics but I think it can definitely be broadened to the soundtrack of Q as a whole. The new slow guitar theme that plays when Shinji is speaking with Rei Q is absolutely hopeless without using any lyrics. And 13's activation was based on Shinji and Kaworu's piano mashup, which didn't really have any lyrics either.
